user_query:
I need a clean set of customer support message templates built from messy text. Please read input/raw_transcripts.jsonl and input/notes.txt. For each record in raw_transcripts.jsonl, take the "text" field and format it so that: (1) the first letter of each sentence is capitalized (based on ". " sentence boundaries), (2) multiple spaces/tabs/newlines are collapsed to a single space and leading/trailing spaces are trimmed, and (3) the message ends with proper sentence-ending punctuation (. ! or ?); add a period if it’s missing. Keep URLs intact and don’t alter abbreviations or brand names mentioned in input/notes.txt.

Deliverables (write all files under output/):
- output/formatted_transcripts.jsonl: one JSON object per line with fields {"id": <same as input>, "formatted_text": <formatted string>} in the same order as the input.
- output/combined_formatted.txt: concatenate all formatted_text values in order, separated by a blank line between entries.
- output/qc_report.md: a brief QA report including: total records processed (label as "Processed:"), a count of how many entries required terminal punctuation to be added (label as "Added terminal punctuation:"), and short notes/assumptions about how you treated abbreviations, URLs, contractions, and any edge cases.

Do not include any other outputs, and keep everything in English.
